🌱 jaeger: Multiarch docker images (#1232)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "jaeger-2292-multiarch-docker-images",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "jaeger: Multiarch docker images",
+    "description": "Multiarch docker images. Requested by 5+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current jaeger deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your jaeger version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger\nhelm list -n observability 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working jaeger installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review jaeger configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant jaeger configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger\nkubectl get configmap -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=jaeger\n```\n## Requirement - what kind of business use case are you trying to solve?\nAbility to utilize multiarch images\n\n## Problem - what in Jaeger blocks you from solving the requirement?\nI have a rpi k3s (arm64) cluster that I use to run a web app written"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Multiarch docker images",
+        "description": "## Which problem is this PR solving?\n- first step to support #2292 \n\n## Short description of the changes\n- `TARGETARCH` is the `ARG` need for `buildx` https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#automatic-platform-args-in-the-global-scope\n\nnow could build local by\n\n```bash\nmake build-ui\nmake\n```yaml\nmake build-ui\nmake build-query GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64\nmake build-all-in-one GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64\nmake build-query GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64\nmake build-all-in-one GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64\nrepo=jaegertracing/all-in-one # all change to custom repo\ndocker buildx build --platform=linux/amd64,linux/arm64 --push -f cmd/all-in-one/Dockerfile -t $repo:latest cmd/all-in-one\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger\nkubectl get events -n observability --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Multiarch docker images\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "## Which problem is this PR solving?\n- first step to support #2292 \n\n## Short description of the changes\n- `TARGETARCH` is the `ARG` need for `buildx` https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#automatic-platform-args-in-the-global-scope\n\nnow could build local by\n\n```bash\nmake build-ui\nmake build-query GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64\nmake build-all-in-one GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64\nmake build-query",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "make build-ui\nmake build-query GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64\nmake build-all-in-one GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64\nmake build-query GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64\nmake build-all-in-one GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64\nrepo=jaegertracing/all-in-one # all change to custom repo\ndocker buildx build --platform=linux/amd64,linux/arm64 --push -f cmd/all-in-one/Dockerfile -t $repo:latest cmd/all-in-one"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "jaeger",
+      "graduated",
+      "observability",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "jaeger"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],

+      "feature"
+    ],

+      "issue": "https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/issues/2292",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger/pull/2320"
+    },
+    "reactions": 5,
+    "comments": 4,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",

+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with jaeger installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
